blurb: Paul's gentle correction here reminds us that the end is not a date to circle on calendars but a presence to recognize in the present. The mystery of lawlessness may seem to rage unchecked, but it operates only by permission of One who holds all times in his hands. While others chase after signs and seasons, we who know the Father's love can rest in the quiet confidence that what has been revealed in Christ is already the decisive word—no deception can overturn the grace that has already made us free.

verses.text: Now we beseech you, brethren, by the coming of our Lord Jesus Christ, and by our gathering together unto him,
That ye be not soon shaken in mind, or be troubled, neither by spirit, nor by word, nor by letter as from us, as that the day of Christ is at hand.
Let no man deceive you by any means: for that day shall not come, except there come a falling away first, and that man of sin be revealed, the son of perdition;
Who opposeth and exalteth himself above all that is called God, or that is worshipped; so that he as God sitteth in the temple of God, shewing himself that he is God.
